finding: Chain resolution algorithm missing — tip computation, orphans, tie-breaker
rationale: §6.5 already defines the algorithm in 5 deterministic steps including orphan handling; ChatGPT missed existing prose

finding: Ordering / timestamp field missing on artefacts — resolver ordering guarantee unstated
rationale: §6.5 resolver did not state its ordering guarantee; added arrival-position and tie-breaker note

finding: relatedArtefactIds too loose — relatedArtefactType semantic hints enum suggested
rationale: BriefArtefactBase field is in briefResultContract.ts on main; out of scope for this spec; contract-revision task

finding: Approval execution edge cases — orphaned execution, timeout→failed, heartbeat unspecified
rationale: Execution lifecycle belongs in docs/brief-result-contract.md on BriefApprovalCard.executionStatus; out of spec scope

finding: Budget context enforcement underspecified — what happens on exceed; pre/post check; ownership
rationale: BriefBudgetContext semantics + enforcement ownership are in briefResultContract.ts; spec already references runCostBreaker; no in-spec gap

finding: filtersApplied: [] guard — empty array must be explicitly emitted vs omitted
rationale: filtersApplied is on BriefStructuredResult in briefResultContract.ts; semantic rules belong there; out of scope

finding: Error code enum not formally defined — unsupported_query, provider_error etc. lack canonical list
rationale: Canonical enum in briefResultContract.ts; added in-spec note in §6.4 directing implementers to keep error codes in sync with contract

finding: Duplicate changelog entry — v1 (this version) and v1 (initial) both present
rationale: No changelog section exists in this spec file; finding targets docs/universal-brief-dev-brief.md which is out of scope

finding: Repetition in RLS section — Primary enforcement clause repeated
rationale: Repetition is intentional defence-in-depth readability; each section is self-contained on purpose; stylistic preference with no functional impact

finding: columns optional but recommended — minimum shape expectation (name + type) not defined
rationale: BriefColumnHint minimum shape belongs in briefResultContract.ts; out of scope for this spec

finding: Partial-chain / multiple candidate tips — resolver behaviour under incomplete local knowledge unspecified
rationale: Real distributed-systems edge case not addressed in §6.5; added Partial-knowledge behaviour paragraph with MUST-render-all / MUST-converge obligations

finding: Execution terminal guarantee — every execution must eventually reach completed or failed
rationale: Execution lifecycle belongs in docs/brief-result-contract.md; consistent with R1-F4 deferral; spec does not own executionStatus semantics

finding: budgetContext descriptive-only / enforcement ownership still slightly unclear
rationale: BriefBudgetContext field semantics in briefResultContract.ts; consistent with R1-F5 deferral; no in-spec gap

finding: source must be surfaced when trust-affecting — rendering obligation unstated
rationale: Client rendering obligation for source is in-spec territory (§8.3); added MUST-surface bullet with stale-canonical and hybrid conditions

finding: v1/v1 duplicate changelog in contract file — still present
rationale: Targets docs/brief-result-contract.md merged to main; out of scope; consistent with R1-F8 reject reasoning (different artefact, same disposition)

title: Hard boundary - logic placement: Automations must not contain control flow
Capability contract for Automations - side_effects + idempotent columns
Failure semantics Workflow-Automation - already covered by 5.7+5.8
Observability gap - already covered by 5.9+9a
Automation lifecycle ownership - versioning+immutable execution+opt-in upgrades
UX contract - Workflows primary construct; Automations supporting capabilities
Capability Layer framing - stylistic duplication of principle 1
Derive-dont-duplicate - Workflows reference Automations by ID
Composition constraints - max nesting depth, no recursive Workflow calls
Marketplace/partner/BYO future-proofing - capability ingestion
automations.deterministic flag - caching/memoisation trigger
Engine-enforced non-idempotent retry guard + overrideNonIdempotentGuard opt-in
automations.expected_duration_class flag - queue prioritisation trigger
Standardised AutomationStepError shape {code,type,message,retryable}
5.10a future-relaxation clause - rules not edited in place
5.4a side_effects enum - value definitions read_only/mutating/unknown
irreversible as third side_effects enum value - auto-gate-bypass trigger
1.5 principle 4 - unknown-safe default
5.4a rule 3 - hard retry ceiling maxAttempts<=3 engine-enforced
5.7 type unknown bucket + orchestration-safety contract
5.10a consolidating statement - authoring+runtime enforcement
1.5 principle 5 - side_effects drives UX no silent downgrades
1.5 principle 2 - contract is source of truth paragraph
5.9 retryAttempt required field - attempt-timeline telemetry
Engine drift from contract - centralise enforcement routed to KNOWLEDGE.md
Thin execution test harness validating 5.4a+5.10a contract behaviour

finding: Failure mode tables for critical components (PrincipalContext, recordIncident, sweep, baseline, triage, synthetic-check)
rationale: Internal contracts scattered across §4.3/§4.4/§9.2; consolidating into §4.7 per-component tables clarifies behaviour on each failure mode

finding: Structured partial-success contract for sweep + synthetic-checks tick
rationale: Partial fail today not formally specified for batch handlers; added SweepResult shape + status enum

finding: Global idempotency invariant + key-format conventions across triage/sweep/synthetic/feedback/baseline/recordIncident
rationale: Idempotency enforced ad-hoc; consolidated §4.8 names key-format conventions + storage + TTL across all externally-triggerable jobs

finding: Concurrency / race-condition rules for triage vs sweep on same incident, baseline overlap, feedback double-submit, throttle map race
rationale: Pieces existed in §9.2/§9.3/§10.4; new §4.9 consolidates with explicit posture per scenario

finding: Structured-log queryable-dimensions invariant
rationale: §12.3 had informal convention; promoted to enforced invariant + named queryable dimensions

finding: Stuck-state detection rule for system_monitor agent itself
rationale: §9.5 detects stuck states in OTHER agents; added §9.11 self-stuck detection for the monitor's own runs

finding: Output contract schema versioning for write_diagnosis / write_event JSON
rationale: Added schema_version: v1 to agent_diagnosis JSON + investigate_prompt_outcome metadata; forward-compat hedge

finding: Migration runtime-rollback / shadow-mode / feature-flag for new schema
rationale: [missing-doc] contradicts spec-context.md staged_rollout: never_for_this_codebase_yet + feature_flags: only_for_behaviour_modes + migration_safety_tests: defer_until_live_data_exists; existing additive-only forward-only-with-down-mate posture is the agreed pattern

finding: Final line must be [GATE] structured field / dual validation
rationale: Spec uses tool-call writes (write_diagnosis) with explicit JSON + separate prompt-validation regex; the structured-write pattern is already in place

finding: One-page glossary for spec-internal vocabulary
rationale: §0A glossary added with 12 terms; cheap clarity win

finding: Explicit defaults — retries / timeouts / failure recovery
rationale: §12.4 consolidated retry counts + timeouts + failure-recovery defaults table

finding: Cross-system interaction contracts (Idempotency × Retry, Concurrency × Idempotency, Partial Success × Retry, Failure × Idempotency)
rationale: §4.10 added with six interaction subsections naming each axis pair explicitly

finding: Idempotency storage + lifecycle + cleanup + collision handling
rationale: §4.8 extended with per-layer storage / lifecycle / collision matrix + no-persistent-store rationale

finding: In-progress state semantics — duplicate-request behaviour while work in flight
rationale: §4.10.6 added; handler-level only via existing singleton-key dedup; explicit no-new-DB-status-column callout

finding: Heartbeat + liveness contract — interval, timeout, recovery
rationale: §4.10.5 names three existing layers (pg-boss / runtime_ms / §9.11 semantic-stuck) + stalled-state recovery; no new primitive

finding: Schema evolution rules — backward / forward compat + old-record readability
rationale: §4.8 schema-evolution rules added; readers tolerate unknown / writers don't drop required without bump / old rows readable indefinitely

finding: Log volume / cost guardrails — sampling rules + always-on tiers
rationale: §12.3 log-level + sampling rules table; errors/warns/state-transitions never sampled; high-frequency info sampled

finding: Monitor agent escalation path — severity ladder (apply) + auto-remediation (reject)
rationale: §9.11 severity ladder added; auto-remediation portion rejected (contradicts §9.11 No auto-recovery + Phase 3 §19.2 scope)

finding: Deterministic ordering guarantees — declare per-path FIFO vs commutative
rationale: §4.9.9 per-path table; every new path must declare its bucket

finding: No silent fallback rule — every fallback emits structured log with reason
rationale: §12.3 invariant added with concrete enforcement points referencing §4.7 failure-mode tables

finding: Idempotency key scope enforcement — operation_type + scope + identity component
rationale: §4.8 structural-rule paragraph added + cross-operation-collision rule + same-key-different-payload rule + no-payload-fingerprinting rationale

finding: Input fingerprinting via canonical JSON serialisation + stable hash
rationale: §4.8 keys constructed from named structured inputs (incidentId/candidateId/bucketKey); canonical by construction; payload-hash would add failure surface for zero benefit

finding: Retry budget + termination rules (max retry / backoff / failed_permanent)
rationale: Already in §12.4 (pg-boss retry=3 + exponential + DLQ as terminal), §9.8 (max 2 retries), §9.9 (rate limit terminal), §4.7.5 (agent_triage_failed at terminal); failed_permanent would duplicate

finding: Partial-success per-pair retryability classification
rationale: §9.3 retryability paragraph added; per-pair classification field rejected (every errored pair structurally retryable on next tick); upstream guardrails are the actual retry bounds

finding: Single source of truth clarification (work-product / events / idempotency / logs)
rationale: §4.10.7 added with 4-layer ranking + when-two-layers-disagree-work-product-wins rule

finding: Time semantics — UTC ISO 8601 server-generated only
rationale: §4.10.8 added; client clocks never authoritative; applied to DB / logs / events / API responses

finding: At-least-once vs exactly-once delivery / outcome model
rationale: §4.10.9 added; at-least-once delivery + effectively-exactly-once outcome via idempotency named explicitly

finding: Backpressure / load-shedding rule (rejected_over_capacity status)
rationale: §4.10.10 added with cap-signal table + no-silent-drop-under-load rule; rejected_over_capacity status rejected (would duplicate cap-signal events per §4.10.7)

finding: Versioned contract for monitor outputs — schema_version + version-branching
rationale: Already in §9.8 (schema_version: v1 on all agent JSON), §12.1 metadata column, §4.8 schema-evolution rules from R2; restating would be over-specification ChatGPT warned about

finding: CI enforceability hook — every invariant MUST have a CI test or gate
rationale: Contradicts spec-context.md runtime_tests: pure_function_only + testing_posture: static_gates_primary; spec already names CI gates where they apply (§4.7.1 RLS, §4.8 idempotency, §14)

finding: Consistency / deduplication pass across §4.8 / §4.10 / §12 / §9.3 / §9.8 / §9.11
rationale: Six possible-duplicate candidates evaluated; zero genuine duplicates; cross-cutting rule (§4.10.x) + local mechanism (§9.x/§12.x) pairs serve different reader paths with explicit cross-references already in place

finding: Implementation Alignment Check — observability of invariants / failure-mode detectability / retry-path traceability
rationale: ChatGPT explicitly framed as non-spec; belongs to spec-conformance + audit phases; already covered structurally via §12.3 queryable-dimensions + §4.7 failure modes + §4.10.1 / §12.4 retry contracts

finding: Real-World Validation — failure simulation / concurrency stress / partial-success replay
rationale: Belongs to build/test phase; §14 testing strategy is the locus; specific test cases are an implementation concern; adding test enumerations contradicts spec-context.md runtime_tests: pure_function_only

finding: Spec finalisation block — Status: Finalised v1 (Execution Ready)
rationale: ## Spec Status section added after §19 with finalisation block (status / last review / total findings / next step)
